source_jobs.title: Backend Engineer (MTA), Core Sending
source_jobs.description:
Resend is growing fast, and there are now over 2 million developers using it. As the Core Sending Squad, we are responsible for building and maintaining the core components of Resend that handle sending transactional and marketing emails.

This role is fully remote-based in Americas or European time zones, with some expected travel for team off-sites, conferences, meetups, and more.

Read more about how we work, how we hire, and what we value here https://resend.com/handbook.




IN THIS ROLE, YOU WILL…

 - Build and operate Resend’s email transport infrastructure

 - Work on SMTP delivery, MTA systems, routing, retries, backoff strategies, and delivery controls

 - Design systems for domain-level, provider-level, customer-level, and IP-level throttling

 - Build infrastructure for shared IP pools, dedicated IPs, warmup execution, and reputation-aware routing

 - Improve how Resend classifies bounces, deferrals, transient failures, hard failures, and delivery outcomes

 - Build tools that help the team debug delivery issues by customer, domain, mailbox provider, route, IP, message type, and event

 - Help design systems that make sending behavior safer, more observable, and easier to operate

 - Translate reputation strategy into backend systems

 - Partner with Trust & Safety to build controls that protect Resend’s sending network from abuse

 - Participate in incident response for sending, provider, and deliverability-sensitive issues

 - Be on-call for critical services




YOU WILL BE A GREAT FIT IF YOU...

 - Have strong backend engineering experience

 - Have worked on email, messaging, notifications, communications, infrastructure, or other high-volume delivery systems

 - Understand queues, workers, retries, idempotency, rate limits, backpressure, and distributed systems

 - Have experience or strong interest in SMTP, MTAs, DNS, bounces, deferrals, DNS, IP pools, and mailbox-provider behavior

 - Care about reliability, observability, operational excellence, and reducing blast radius

 - Have low ego, high ownership, and strong taste for simple systems




YOU WILL BE AN EXCEPTIONAL FIT IF YOU ALSO...

 - Have experience working on applications with a high workload

 - Know about email best practices, or are willing to learn more about this space

 - Are experienced in Typescript and Golang


WHAT IT MEANS TO JOIN THE TEAM:

 - Autonomy to "just ship it"

 - 100% remote team with flexible working schedules

 - Modern tech stack (Typescript, AWS, Go, Postgres, Datadog)

 - Honest and low-ego team

 - Ownership of problems and solutions

 - Comp: $150,000 - $170,000 USD, commensurate with experience





ABOUT RESEND

We are building the modern email sending platform for developers. We care deeply about quality, craft, and the developer experience.

Our fully remote team of 45 spans 13 countries and counting. We're backed by a16z, Y Combinator, and others. Meet the humans → https://resend.com/humans

source_jobs.title: Deliverability Engineer, Core Sending
source_jobs.description:
Resend is growing fast, and there are now over 2 million developers using it. As the Core Sending Squad, we are responsible for building and maintaining the core components of Resend that handle sending transactional and marketing emails.

This role is fully remote-based in Americas time zones, with some expected travel for team off-sites, conferences, meetups, and more.

Read more about how we work, how we hire, and what we value here https://resend.com/handbook.




YOU WILL BE A GREAT FIT IF YOU...

 - Have experience in email deliverability, reputation, mail operations, anti-abuse, or high-volume email systems

 - Understand IP reputation, domain reputation, authentication, bounces, complaints, suppressions, and warmup

 - Know how major mailbox providers behave and how to diagnose provider-specific delivery issues

 - Can turn ambiguous deliverability judgment into metrics, policies, runbooks, dashboards, and systems

 - Communicate clearly with engineering, support, product, customers

 - Have low ego, strong judgment, and high ownership




YOU WILL BE AN EXCEPTIONAL FIT IF YOU ALSO...

 - Have operated shared and dedicated IP pools

 - Have designed or managed IP warmup programs

 - Have recovered from provider blocks, spam-foldering issues, complaint spikes, deferral spikes, or blocklist events

 - Have experience with Google Postmaster Tools, Microsoft SNDS/JMRP, Yahoo Sender Hub, feedback loops, or blocklist monitoring

 - Understand DKIM, SPF, DMARC, PTR, TLS, DSNs, List-Unsubscribe, suppression, and bounce classification

 - Understand IP reputation, domain reputation, authentication, bounces, complaints, suppressions, and warmup

 - Have partnered with Trust & Safety, anti-abuse, support, or customer-facing teams

 - Have helped build deliverability dashboards, reputation scoring, traffic segmentation, or automated enforcement systems

 - Have experience helping engineering teams turn deliverability knowledge into product and infrastructure




WHAT IT MEANS TO JOIN THE TEAM:

 - Autonomy to "just ship it"

 - 100% remote team with flexible working schedules

 - Modern tech stack (AWS, Next.js, Raycast, Notion, etc.)

 - Honest and low-ego team

 - Ownership of problems and solutions

 - Comp: $150,000 - $180,000 USD, commensurate with experience




ABOUT RESEND

We are building the modern email sending platform for developers. We care deeply about quality, craft, and the developer experience.

Our fully remote team of 45 spans 13 countries and counting. We're backed by a16z, Y Combinator, and others. Meet the humans → https://resend.com/humans

source_jobs.title: Product Engineer
source_jobs.description:
Resend is growing fast, and there are now over 2 million developers using it. As a Product Engineer, you will help shape the core product experience of Resend.

This role is for someone who cares about both how the product works and how the product feels. You should be comfortable moving between frontend, backend, product thinking, design details, and user experience.

This role is fully remote based in Americas or European timezones, with some expected travel for team offsites, conferences, meetups, and more.

Read more about how we work, how we hire, and what we value here https://resend.com/handbook.




IN THIS ROLE, YOU WILL…

 - Build polished product experiences for users using Resend.

 - Turn complex technical concepts into simple, clear, and trustworthy user experiences.

 - Own features end-to-end: product thinking, UX details, implementation, instrumentation, launch, and iteration.

 - Collaborate closely with design, engineering, and customer success to understand user problems and ship high-quality solutions.

 - Build debugging and observability experiences that help users understand what happened to their emails, webhooks, domains, and API requests.

 - Work primarily with TypeScript, React, Next.js, and modern web technologies.

 - Talk to users, understand customer pain, and turn those insights into better product decisions.

 - Help raise the bar for craft, performance, accessibility, reliability, and product quality across Resend.




YOU WILL BE A GREAT FIT IF YOU...

 - Have 6+ years of experience building and shipping high-quality web products.

 - Are fluent in writing and speaking English.

 - Have strong experience with TypeScript, React, and modern frontend development.

 - Are comfortable working across the stack, including APIs, databases, server-side logic, and product infrastructure.

 - Care deeply about UX, interaction details, and product polish.

 - Can take an ambiguous customer problem and turn it into a clear, shippable solution.

 - Have strong product judgment

 - Love helping developers and care about developer experience

 - Are comfortable working in open-ended problems and situations

 - Have a “default to action” mindset

 - Are hands-on and enjoy solving problems directly

 - Have low ego and enjoy collaborating with design, engineering, and customer success




YOU WILL BE AN EXCEPTIONAL FIT IF YOU ALSO...

 - Have built products for developers, infrastructure teams, or technical users

 - Have experience with email, deliverability, DNS, webhooks, logs, observability, or API platforms

 - Have strong visual taste and can build interfaces that feel fast, simple, and polished

 - Have experience working in small, high-ownership teams




WHAT IT MEANS TO JOIN THE TEAM:

 - Autonomy to "just ship it"

 - 100% remote team with flexible working schedules

 - Modern tech stack (Next.js, Raycast, Notion, etc.)

 - Honest and low-ego team

 - Ownership of problems and solutions

 - Comp: $150,000 - $170,000 USD, commensurate with experience




ABOUT RESEND

We are building the modern email sending platform for developers. We care deeply about quality, craft, and the developer experience.

Our fully remote team of 45 spans 13 countries and counting. We're backed by a16z, Y Combinator, and others. Meet the humans → https://resend.com/humans

source_jobs.title: Security Engineer, Platform
source_jobs.description:
Resend is growing fast, and there are now over 2 million developers using it. As the Platform Squad, we are responsible for the internal platform and reliability foundations that let Resend teams build, deploy, observe, and operate services safely.

This role is fully remote based in Americas or European timezones, with some expected travel for team offsites, conferences, meetups, and more.

Read more about how we work, how we hire, and what we value here https://resend.com/handbook.




IN THIS ROLE, YOU WILL…

 - Build and improve the security foundations of Resend’s platform, including API keys, service permissions, secrets management, tenant isolation, audit logs, and internal access controls

 - Help design secure defaults for new services, workers, queues, databases, internal tools, and deployment pipelines

 - Improve detection and response for suspicious access, leaked credentials, abnormal sending behavior, privilege changes, and sensitive system events

 - Review and update our processes/pipelines so that security is “built-in”, not “bolt-on”

 - Raise the security bar




YOU WILL BE A GREAT FIT IF YOU...

 - Have experience securing production infrastructure, cloud environments, APIs, developer platforms, or multi-tenant SaaS products

 - Can write code and are comfortable reading application, infrastructure, and deployment code

 - Understand authentication, authorization, secrets, IAM, logging, audit trails, incident response, and secure deployment pipelines.

 - Independently prioritize and drive your day-to-day as the first dedicated security hire

 - Prefer practical improvements over heavy processes

 - Are low-ego, direct, curious, and willing to learn from others

 - Care about developer experience and believe security should be easy to adopt




YOU WILL BE AN EXCEPTIONAL FIT IF YOU ALSO...

 - Have experience with email infrastructure, transactional email, sender reputation, domain verification, SPF, DKIM, DMARC, webhooks, or abuse prevention

 - Have secured cloud infrastructure using tools like AWS, Terraform, Kubernetes, Cloudflare, or similar systems

 - Have experience with open source security, GitHub organization hardening, package publishing, dependency review, or supply-chain security

 - Have helped a company prepare for SOC 2, ISO 27001, GDPR, enterprise security reviews, or customer trust processes without turning engineering into a compliance machine

 - Have built security observability, alerting, detection pipelines, or incident response workflows

 - Have worked closely with Trust & Safety, anti-abuse, fraud, or platform integrity teams

 - Have experience designing security systems for high-scale developer products




WHAT IT MEANS TO JOIN THE TEAM:

 - Autonomy to "just ship it"

 - 100% remote team with flexible working schedules

 - Modern tech stack (Next.js, Raycast, Notion, etc.)

 - Honest and low-ego team

 - Ownership of problems and solutions

 - Comp: $120,000 - $140,000 USD, commensurate with experience




ABOUT RESEND

We are building the modern email sending platform for developers. We care deeply about quality, craft, and the developer experience.

Our fully remote team of 45 spans 13 countries and counting. We're backed by a16z, Y Combinator, and others. Meet the humans → https://resend.com/humans

source_jobs.title: Backend Engineer (Infra), Core Sending
source_jobs.description:
Resend is growing fast, and there are now over 2 million developers using it. As the Core Sending Squad, we are responsible for building and maintaining the core components of Resend that handle sending transactional and marketing emails.

This role is fully remote-based in Americas or European time zones, with some expected travel for team off-sites, conferences, meetups, and more.

Read more about how we work, how we hire, and what we value here https://resend.com/handbook.




IN THIS ROLE, YOU WILL…

 - Help define the architecture to support millions of daily API requests

 - Build and scale infrastructure to send dozens of millions of emails per day

 - Improve high availability across distributed applications

 - Scale databases like Postgres and Clickhouse to ensure that they are performant

 - Enhance observability by using monitoring tools like Datadog

 - Refine disaster recovery plans to ensure that recovery is quick and reliable

 - Help to build our infrastructure with IaC frameworks like CDK and TF

 - Work with Typescript and Golang

 - Design and operate async pipelines (queues, streams, idempotency, retries, DLQs) that move tens of millions of messages per day

 - Be on-call rotation (for critical services)




YOU WILL BE A GREAT FIT IF YOU...

 - 8+ years of back-end engineering with a focus on cloud infrastructure

 - Are fluent in writing and speaking English

 - Thrive in open-ended problems and situations

 - Have supported large workloads on AWS

 - You love helping other developers

 - “Default to action” kind of person

 - Hands-on on solving problems

 - Experience with event-driven systems at scale. Kafka, SQS/SNS, or similar.

 - Low ego




YOU WILL BE AN EXCEPTIONAL FIT IF YOU ALSO...

 - Have experience working on applications with a high workload

 - Know about email best practices, or are willing to learn more about this space

 - Are experienced in Typescript and Golang




WHAT IT MEANS TO JOIN THE TEAM:

 - Autonomy to "just ship it"

 - 100% remote team with flexible working schedules

 - Modern tech stack

 - Honest and low-ego team

 - Ownership of problems and solutions

 - Comp: $150,000 - $170,000 USD, commensurate with experience





ABOUT RESEND

We are building the modern email sending platform for developers. We care deeply about quality, craft, and the developer experience.

Our fully remote team of 45 spans 13 countries and counting. We're backed by a16z, Y Combinator, and others. Meet the humans → https://resend.com/humans
